The Vajpayee government faced a massive, highly volatile political crisis that threatened to completely shatter the ruling National Democratic Alliance (NDA). ## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On March 30, 1867, United States Secretary of State William H. Seward achieved a massive, highly monumental diplomatic and territorial acquisition by officially signing the treaty to purchase Alaska from the Russian Empire for a mere $7.2 million. Widely and aggressively mocked at the time by the American press as "Seward's Folly," the massive, incredibly vast territory was later discovered to contain massive, unimaginable wealth in gold, copper, and oil, fundamentally transforming the massive strategic and economic posture of the United States.

On March 30, 1981, US President Ronald Reagan miraculously survived a massive, highly aggressive assassination attempt in Washington, D.C. John Hinckley Jr., seeking to aggressively impress a Hollywood actress, fired six shots, severely wounding the President, his press secretary, and two law enforcement officers. Reagan's massive, highly publicized recovery and aggressive, good-humored resilience in the face of death fundamentally cemented his massive, enduring popularity and heavily defined his incredibly powerful, two-term political legacy.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On March 30, 1919, the British colonial administration passed the massive, incredibly draconian Rowlatt Act (The Anarchical and Revolutionary Crimes Act). The legislation heavily allowed the government to aggressively imprison any Indian suspected of terrorism without trial or judicial review for up to two years. The massive, deeply oppressive act triggered absolute, nationwide fury and directly catalyzed Mahatma Gandhi's massive Rowlatt Satyagraha, heavily setting the stage for the horrific Jallianwala Bagh massacre just weeks later.

On March 30, 1949, the massive, highly historic political integration of the massive Rajputana states was heavily formalized. The major, powerful princely states of Jaipur, Jodhpur, Bikaner, and Jaisalmer formally merged to heavily form the massive, new state of Rajasthan within the Indian Union. This massive, diplomatic triumph, aggressively spearheaded by Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, fundamentally unified the incredibly vast, historically fragmented desert region into the absolute, largest geographical state in the modern Republic of India.
